skill.name: azure-microsoft-playwright-testing-ts
skill.description: Configure Azure Playwright Workspaces for TypeScript Playwright projects, including cloud-hosted browsers, Entra ID authentication, CI integration, and migration from the retired Microsoft Playwright Testing package.
skill.summary: Configure TypeScript Playwright tests to run at scale on Azure Playwright Workspaces.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command-execution patterns, network URLs, credential references, a weak-crypto alert, and a critical heuristic combination. Review found these are Markdown documentation examples for npm, Azure CLI, Playwright, CI pipelines, service URLs, and secret placeholders; no bundled script, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, or obfuscation was found. The skill is publishable with low operational risk because users may copy commands that interact with Azure services and CI secrets.

low_findings.title: Markdown command examples require user review
low_findings.description: The external command findings are TypeScript, Bash, GitHub Actions, and Azure Pipelines examples in documentation. They do not execute when the skill is installed, but copied commands can install packages, log in to Azure, and run cloud tests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious patterns are inside Markdown code fences and CI examples, not standalone executable files. The operational impact is real only if a user chooses to copy and run them.

low_findings.title: Credential references are secret placeholders
low_findings.description: The environment access findings point to CI secret placeholders for Azure client, tenant, subscription, and Playwright service values. No hardcoded secret value or exfiltration target was found, but users must configure least-privilege credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows standard CI secret interpolation syntax and Azure service configuration. I found no literal credential values or instructions to transmit secrets to an unrelated endpoint.

low_findings.title: Network URLs are expected documentation and test targets
low_findings.description: The network findings include an Azure migration guide, an Azure Playwright service endpoint placeholder, and example.com in a sample test. These references match the skill purpose and do not indicate hidden data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each URL appears in visible documentation context and aligns with Azure Playwright setup or a neutral Playwright example. No evidence found of covert networking or data collection.

low_findings.title: Weak crypto and critical heuristic alerts were not confirmed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to descriptive frontmatter, not cryptographic code. The critical combination heuristic is explained by visible documentation for commands, Azure service URLs, and CI secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain skill metadata, installation examples, and CI secret placeholders. I found no cryptographic implementation, encoded payload, hidden instruction, or malicious data flow.

content.value_statement: Large Playwright suites can become slow or hard to report across teams. This skill guides Azure Playwright Workspaces setup for cloud browsers, CI runs, authentication, reporting, and package migration.
actual_capabilities: Shows TypeScript configuration using createAzurePlaywrightConfig and ServiceOS.
Documents Entra ID and managed identity authentication patterns.
Provides GitHub Actions and Azure Pipelines examples for cloud test runs.
Describes Azure reporter setup and manual browser connection options.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not provision Azure Playwright Workspaces.
Users must supply valid Azure credentials, workspace URLs, and CI secrets.
Version guidance may need verification against current Azure SDK releases.
It focuses on TypeScript Playwright projects, not other test frameworks.

use_cases.title: Move a local Playwright suite to Azure
use_cases.description: Configure cloud-hosted browsers, service URLs, workers, and authentication for a TypeScript Playwright project.

use_cases.title: Add cloud test execution to CI
use_cases.description: Use the provided GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines patterns to run Playwright tests against Azure infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Migrate from the retired package
use_cases.description: Replace old Microsoft Playwright Testing APIs with @azure/playwright names, options, and reporter configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic Azure Playwright setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a TypeScript Playwright project
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure a TypeScript Playwright project to use Azure Playwright Workspaces with Entra ID authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Review my service config
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Playwright service configuration for Azure Playwright Workspaces. Check authentication, OS selection, reporter order, and worker settings.

prompt_templates.title: Build a CI workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Add cloud Playwright runs to CI
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CI plan for running Playwright tests on Azure Playwright Workspaces using secret-based configuration and least-privilege Azure credentials.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a package migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my current @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing usage with @azure/playwright and produce a migration checklist for configuration, authentication, reporter changes, and validation.

output_examples.input: Configure Azure Playwright Workspaces for my TypeScript tests.
output_examples.output: A setup checklist covering package installation, workspace URL configuration, Entra ID credential selection, service config changes, and a first cloud test run.

output_examples.input: Add Azure Playwright tests to GitHub Actions.
output_examples.output: A CI integration plan that identifies required secrets, Azure login permissions, install steps, worker settings, and test reporting expectations.

output_examples.input: Migrate from the old Microsoft Playwright Testing package.
output_examples.output: A migration summary that maps old APIs to new @azure/playwright APIs and calls out authentication and reporter changes.

best_practices: Use Entra ID or managed identity instead of long-lived access tokens when possible.
Store workspace URLs and Azure identifiers in CI secrets or protected variables.
Keep local HTML reporting enabled before Azure reporter output for easier debugging.
anti_patterns: Do not commit Azure client secrets, tenant IDs, or service URLs directly in source files.
Do not run high worker counts without checking Azure quota and application capacity.
Do not keep retired package imports after migrating to @azure/playwright.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides setup guidance and command examples that users run in their own project or CI system.

faq.question: Which authentication method does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Microsoft Entra ID with DefaultAzureCredential or managed identity patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a GitHub Actions pattern using Azure login and protected repository secrets.

faq.question: Can it help with Azure Pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes an AzureCLI task pattern with a service connection and Playwright service URL variable.

faq.question: Does it cover migration from @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists package, API, option, credential, and reporter changes for the new @azure/playwright package.

faq.question: What should users verify before applying the examples?
faq.answer: Users should verify package versions, Azure workspace settings, credential scope, CI permissions, and current Microsoft documentation.
